title: [Dockerfile] Consume nodes.lock — pin ComfyUI core + every custom node SHA
description:
**Scope:** update serverless-docker/Dockerfile:

1. ComfyUI core clone: 'git clone <url> /ComfyUI && git -C /ComfyUI checkout <sha-from-lock>'.
2. Custom node loop: COPY the lockfile into the build context, parse it, for each line clone the repo and checkout the SHA before running requirements.txt/install.py.
3. Add CI-time assertion: every repo listed in Dockerfile's install array must have a matching entry in nodes.lock (fail build if mismatched).

**Acceptance:**
- v<N+1> built after lockfile is generated produces an image whose 'git -C /ComfyUI rev-parse HEAD' (and same for each custom_nodes/*) matches nodes.lock exactly.
- Build still succeeds with the current set of nodes.

**Blocked by:** freeze script must exist first.

title: [freeze] Script to snapshot local SHAs into serverless-docker/nodes.lock
description:
**Scope:** new script (probably bash or python, at serverless-docker/scripts/freeze.sh or .py) that:

1. Reads the Dockerfile to extract the canonical custom_nodes repo list.
2. For each repo, reads SHA from ~/src/comfy/ComfyUI/custom_nodes/<dir>/.git (via 'git -C ... rev-parse HEAD') if present.
3. For Dockerfile-listed repos NOT present locally, falls back to 'git ls-remote <url> HEAD' and warns.
4. Also captures ComfyUI core SHA from ~/src/comfy/ComfyUI/.git.
5. Emits serverless-docker/nodes.lock — one line per entry: '<repo_url> <sha>' (whitespace-separated, sorted by repo for diff-ability).
6. WARNS (not fails) on:
   - Local-only nodes not in Dockerfile (lists them with 'consider adding to Dockerfile if used in workflows')
   - Dockerfile-listed nodes missing locally (used remote HEAD fallback)
7. Idempotent — re-running with no local changes produces byte-identical lockfile.

**Acceptance:**
- 'bash freeze.sh' runs from serverless-docker/ and writes nodes.lock to the same dir.
- Lockfile format documented in CLAUDE.md.
- Tests cover: happy path, missing-local node, malformed Dockerfile, .git missing in local node dir.

**Blocked by:** none (foundational).

title: EPIC: Eliminate version-drift errors from releases (local↔docker pin sync)
description:
**Goal:** every workflow you author locally validates against the docker image built from that same moment in time, every time. No more 'Required input is missing' or 'Value not in list' surprises caused by docker pulling a newer node main than your local has.

**Root causes observed in v18/v19:**
1. Dockerfile uses 'git clone --depth 1' for ComfyUI core + every custom node → each docker build pulls latest main. Two custom nodes (OpenRouterNode, DrawMaskOnImage) added new required inputs in the last ~14 days; workflows authored before that now fail validation.
2. ONNX dropdown bug in kijai/ComfyUI-WanAnimatePreprocess (issue #32, open) — cache-ordering race in folder_paths. controlnet_aux loads alphabetically before WanAnimatePreprocess and warms the cache without .onnx.
3. 4 local-only nodes (GGUF, SCAIL-Pose, tensorops, eulerflow-discrete-scheduler) aren't shipped — workflows using them fail at runtime.
4. 12 docker-only nodes ship but aren't authored against — bloat + cache-ordering risks.

**Design (locked):**
- **Source of truth:** local → docker. Lockfile generated FROM local, consumed BY docker build.
- **Granularity:** per-node SHA + ComfyUI core SHA. No python deps freeze (too much churn for now).
- **Bump trigger:** on-demand. User runs 'freeze' before tagging.
- **List authority:** Dockerfile canonical for what ships; freeze fills SHAs from local where available, ls-remote main as fallback for docker-only nodes.

**Acceptance:**
- 'make freeze' (or equivalent) reads local SHAs, writes nodes.lock to serverless-docker, warns on local-only nodes not in Dockerfile.
- Dockerfile build consumes nodes.lock + checks out exact SHAs (ComfyUI core + every custom node).
- Smoke gate detects when a workflow's class_types aren't installed in the image — fail early, not at runtime.
- Documented release flow in CLAUDE.md.

**Cross-references:** docker repo Hearmeman24/remote-comfy-gen-docker; ComfyGen Hearmeman24/ComfyGen; existing CI gate (bead 9ws, closed).

title: Migrate CircleCI smoke_preset.py from GPU comfy-gen download to CPU install-preset
description:
# Migrate CircleCI smoke from `comfy-gen download` (GPU) to `comfy-gen install-preset` (CPU pod)

Closes the CI half of the cost-cut started in [[remote_comfy_generator-5f2]].

## Rationale

`automation/smoke_preset.py` (called by CircleCI's `smoke_test` job per preset
on every `v<N>` tag) still uses the pre-5f2 path:

```python
# smoke_preset.py:148 — current
subprocess.run([
    "comfy-gen", "download",
    "--batch", dl_file,
    "--endpoint-id", ENDPOINT_ID,  # 5fkobbksgrtww6 — GPU serverless
    ...
])
```

That dispatches the model download to the **GPU serverless worker** — burns
roughly $1.50 of H100 time per preset per release tag, just to run `aria2c`.
Now that `comfy-gen install-preset` (bead 5f2) is on `main` and ships the
CPU-pod path end-to-end, smoke should use it too. Same volume gets the same
files — the only difference is which kind of pod does the writing.

Cost win: ~$1.50 → ~$0.005 per preset per `v<N>` tag (≈300× cheaper). With
2 presets in the live manifest today (`qwen-image-lighting`, `wan-animate`)
that's $3 → $0.01 per release.

## What changes

### `automation/smoke_preset.py`

1. Replace the `comfy-gen download --batch` block (around line 143-160) with
   a `comfy-gen install-preset --preset-id <id> --volume-id <vid>` invocation.
2. Drop `build_downloads(preset)` (line 82) — `install-preset` resolves the
   preset manifest itself via `preset_resolver`.
3. Drop the `/tmp/smoke-<id>-downloads.json` batch file path entirely.
4. Add a helper `resolve_volume_for_endpoint(endpoint_id) -> str` that does
   `GET https://rest.runpod.io/v1/endpoints/<ep>` and returns the first
   `networkVolumeIds[0]`. Cache in module-global so it runs once per smoke
   job.
5. Parse the new stdout — `install-preset` emits **line-delimited JSON
   events**, not one final JSON blob. The existing `_run_comfygen(...)` helper
   (line 101) returns parsed-JSON-stdout assuming the old shape; either:
   - branch on subcommand inside `_run_comfygen`, **or**
   - write a small `_run_install_preset(...)` helper that consumes the event
     stream line-by-line, asserts `install_done.ok == true`, and returns the
     `files` array from the final event.
6. Keep the summary log line shape — `{downloads: {total, cached, fresh}}`
   is reconstructable from the `download_done` events (count `cached: true`
   vs `cached: false`).

### `serverless-docker/.circleci/generate_continue.py`

No required change. `smoke_test` already runs `install-comfygen` (clones
`main` and `pip install`s), so the new `install-preset` subcommand will be
available after this bead lands on ComfyGen `main`. But:

- Remove the FlashBoot-only comment in `smoke_test`'s description if it
  implies "uses the worker to download" — the only thing GPU-serverless
  does in smoke now is execute the workflow (which still needs a worker).
- Optional doc tweak: the smoke_test job no longer waits on
  `wait_for_rollout` solely to verify the worker can run aria2c; it now
  only depends on the worker being up to *run the workflow*.

### Maybe needed: smoke `--volume-id` arg

`smoke_preset.py` only takes `--endpoint-id` today. Two options:

- **A (recommended):** resolve volume from endpoint inside the script. One
  REST call. Zero changes to CircleCI config.
- **B:** add `--volume-id` to `smoke_preset.py` and hard-code it in
  `generate_continue.py` next to `ENDPOINT_ID`. Slightly faster (no REST
  hop) but adds a second magic constant that drifts.

A unless you have a reason.

## TDD plan

### Task 1 — `_run_install_preset` helper

- Failing test in `tests/test_smoke_preset.py` (it exists per the
  serverless-runtime test list): mock `subprocess.Popen` to yield a
  canned `install-preset` event stream → helper returns the file list +
  cached/fresh counts.
- Failing test: stream contains `install_error` → helper raises with the
  reason.
- Failing test: stream contains `preflight_fail` → helper raises with the
  reason (no `install_done` event ever arrives).
- Failing test: subprocess exit 1 with no terminal event → helper raises
  "unexpected exit".
- Implement the helper, run tests, expect pass.

### Task 2 — `resolve_volume_for_endpoint`

- Failing test: mocks `urllib.request.urlopen` returning an endpoint with
  `networkVolumeIds: ["abc"]` → returns `"abc"`.
- Failing test: endpoint has empty `networkVolumeIds` → raises with a
  clear message ("endpoint has no network volume attached").
- Failing test: endpoint has >1 volume → returns the first one + logs a
  warning to stderr.
- Implement, expect pass.

### Task 3 — wire into the main flow

- Replace the `comfy-gen download` block with `_run_install_preset(...)`.
- Drop `build_downloads`, drop `/tmp/smoke-*-downloads.json`.
- Failing integration test (still mocked at subprocess boundary) that
  drives `main(preset_id="qwen-image-lighting", endpoint_id="...")`
  through happy path: stream consumed → workflow submitted → success.

### Task 4 — live verification

- Tag `v<N+1>` on `serverless-docker` after this bead's PR is on main
  (small, no-op image change is fine; the purpose is to fire the smoke
  matrix and verify cost moves from ~$1.50/preset to ~$0.005/preset).
- Confirm in CircleCI logs that the smoke step shows `pod_spawned` and
  `install_done` events, not `Downloading 1/N` lines.
- Confirm in the RunPod billing dashboard that the spawned pod was a
  CPU pod (`costPerHr` ≈ $0.06).

## Acceptance

- `automation/smoke_preset.py` no longer calls `comfy-gen download`.
- New helper consumes line-delimited JSON events.
- Test suite in `tests/test_smoke_preset.py` covers happy path + 3 error
  paths + volume resolution edge cases.
- One `v<N>` smoke run on `main` completes and shows CPU pod cost per
  preset.
- Smoke runtime per preset stays within ~5 min (CPU pod download is
  parallelizable per file via aria2c, same as before — but no rollout
  warm-up cost).

## Out of scope

- Migrating BlockFlow's `preset_routes.py` install path — tracked in
  [[sgs-ui-8ww]].
- Adding GPU fallback when no CPU capacity. The CLI fails loudly today;
  if CI starts seeing capacity failures in practice we'll add fallback
  in the CLI itself.
- Per-volume parallel install (smoke is one-volume-per-endpoint by
  construction).

## Dependencies

- **Blocks on:** [[remote_comfy_generator-5f2]] merged to main on all
  three repos. **Done as of 2026-05-24.**
- After this lands, `serverless-docker/.circleci/generate_continue.py`
  doesn't need a tag bump in lockstep — `smoke_test` clones ComfyGen
  `main` at job start, so the new behavior ships the moment this bead's
  PR is on ComfyGen main.

title: Installer pod: addressable HTTP server (port 3000) with SSE progress + multi-op API
description:
# Installer pod: addressable HTTP server with SSE progress

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: superpowers:subagent-driven-development.
> TDD doctrine in bd memories applies — failing test first, real behavior in tests, edge cases mandatory.
> Builds on [[remote_comfy_generator-ixd]] (shipped 2026-05-24 — one-shot CPU installer pod).

**Goal:** Convert the installer pod from a one-shot `PRESET_ID`-env batch job into an addressable mini-service on port 3000. BlockFlow spawns the pod, waits for `/health`, then drives it via REST (preflight → install → shutdown) and streams progress via SSE — eliminating the "no log API" problem from ixd entirely.

**Why now:** ixd shipped successfully (qwen-install in 56s for $0.0019) but BlockFlow has no way to surface progress to the user — RunPod has no pod-logs API, only a dashboard URL. An in-pod HTTP server gives BlockFlow a real progress stream, lets it preflight free space before committing, and supports multi-op pods (install preset A, then B, without paying two cold starts).

---

## Decision tree (locked via session 2026-05-24)

| Q | Decision | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Where does the server live | Inside the existing `serverless-runtime` repo as `installer_server.py` — shipped via the same `git clone` the pod already does on boot | One source of truth, no new repo, no Dockerfile changes needed for server logic updates |
| Port | 3000, exposed via RunPod's HTTP proxy at `https://<pod_id>-3000.proxy.runpod.net/` | RunPod's standard proxy pattern; HTTPS terminated by RunPod |
| Auth | `X-Installer-Token` header, secret generated at pod-spawn time and injected as `INSTALLER_TOKEN` env | RunPod's proxy is unauthenticated by default; anyone with the URL can hit the server otherwise |
| Web framework | aiohttp (stdlib-adjacent, async-native SSE, no codegen) | FastAPI brings pydantic+uvicorn; overkill for ~4 endpoints. aiohttp is one pip install |
| Backward compat | Keep one-shot env-driven mode from ixd as fallback when `INSTALLER_MODE=oneshot` or `PRESET_ID` is set at spawn | Preserves ixd success path for CI/cron use cases |
| Default mode | Server mode (no `PRESET_ID` at spawn = boot server) | New behavior is the primary; ixd-style is the escape hatch |
| Watchdog | Server self-terminates if no request in 5 min (configurable via `INSTALLER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SEC`) | Prevents abandoned pods from idle-billing forever if BlockFlow crashes between spawn and first call |
| Image | Reuse `Dockerfile.installer`, add `aiohttp` to pip install | No new image needed — same `installer-v<N>` tag pattern |

---

## File structure

### NEW: `serverless-runtime/installer_server.py`
~150 lines. aiohttp app with these endpoints:

| Method | Path | Auth | Body | Response |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GET | `/health` | none | — | `{"ok": true, "version": "<pyproject>", "ready": true}` |
| GET | `/volume_info` | token | — | Delegates to `volume_info_handler.handle()` |
| POST | `/install/{preset_id}` | token | `{"civitai_token": "<opt>", "hf_token": "<opt>"}` | **SSE stream** of `{type, ...}` events |
| POST | `/shutdown` | token | — | `{"ok": true, "terminating": true}` then DELETEs self if `RUNPOD_API_KEY` env set |

**SSE event shapes (one event per line, `data: <json>\n\n`):**
```jsonc
{"type": "preflight_start"}
{"type": "preflight_ok", "preset_id": "...", "models_count": 4, "total_bytes": 52000000000, "volume_free_bytes": 800000000000}
{"type": "preflight_fail", "reason": "..."}                  // insufficient space, preset not found
{"type": "download_start", "file_index": 0, "file": "...", "bytes": 40861031488}
{"type": "download_progress", "file_index": 0, "percent": 45, "speed_bytes_sec": 52428800}
{"type": "download_done", "file_index": 0, "cached": false, "sha256": "..."}
{"type": "install_done", "ok": true, "files": [...], "elapsed_sec": 56}
{"type": "install_error", "reason": "...", "stage": "..."}   // any handler exception
```

**Reuses existing handler logic:**
- `/volume_info` → calls `volume_info_handler.handle({"input": {"command": "volume_info"}})`
- `/install/{preset_id}` → fetches manifest + preset.json (same code currently in `install-entrypoint.sh`, **lift into a Python module** `serverless-runtime/preset_resolver.py` so both the server and the legacy oneshot mode share it), then invokes `download_handler.handle()` but with a progress-callback hook (see next section)

### MODIFY: `serverless-runtime/download_handler.py`
Add an optional `progress_callback: Callable[[dict], None] = None` parameter to `handle()` that, when set, is invoked with structured events instead of (or in addition to) `runpod.serverless.progress_update`. The aiohttp server passes a callback that puts events onto an `asyncio.Queue` consumed by the SSE generator.

```python
def handle(job: dict, progress_callback: Callable[[dict], None] | None = None) -> dict:
    ...
    # at each progress point:
    if progress_callback:
        progress_callback({"type": "download_progress", "file_index": i, "percent": pct, ...})
    else:
        _send_progress(job, ...)  # legacy harness path
```

**Backwards compatibility:** all existing callers (worker dispatch, CLI mode, tests) pass no callback → behavior unchanged. All 16 existing download_handler tests must continue to pass.

### NEW: `serverless-runtime/preset_resolver.py`
~30 lines. Extracted from `install-entrypoint.sh`:
```python
def resolve_preset(preset_id: str, manifest_url: str = DEFAULT_MANIFEST_URL) -> dict:
    """Fetch manifest, look up preset_id, fetch preset.json, return parsed preset dict."""

def preset_to_download_batch(preset: dict) -> list[dict]:
    """Translate preset.models into the download_handler batch shape."""
```

### MODIFY: `serverless-docker/Dockerfile.installer`
Add `aiohttp` to the pip install line. No other changes.

### MODIFY: `serverless-docker/install-entrypoint.sh`
Branch on mode:
```bash
if [ -n "${PRESET_ID:-}" ] || [ "${INSTALLER_MODE:-server}" = "oneshot" ]; then
    # Existing ixd one-shot path — unchanged
    ...
else
    # New server mode
    exec python -m installer_server --port 3000 --token "$INSTALLER_TOKEN"
fi
```

### NEW: ComfyGen CLI command `install-preset`
`comfy_gen/cli/install_preset.py` — wraps the spawn/poll/install/shutdown dance.

```
comfy-gen install-preset --preset-id <id> --volume-id <vid> [--keep-alive] [--image installer-v4]
  # Spawns pod, waits for /health, calls /install, streams SSE to stdout as JSON lines,
  # then POSTs /shutdown (unless --keep-alive). Returns 0 on install_done.ok, 1 otherwise.

comfy-gen install-call --pod-id <pid> --token <t> --preset-id <id>
  # For BlockFlow's multi-op flow: pod already exists, just call /install and stream.
```

Stdout shape (line-delimited JSON, one event per line — same shape as SSE events). BlockFlow can pipe stdout into its UI directly.

### MODIFY: ComfyGen `tests/` — new test files
- `tests/test_installer_server.py` — exercises the server with a mocked download_handler. Real aiohttp test client.
- `tests/test_preset_resolver.py` — mocks urlopen, asserts manifest+preset.json parsing.
- `tests/test_download_handler.py` — add cases for `progress_callback` parameter (3 new tests; existing 16 must still pass).
- `tests/test_install_preset_cli.py` — mocks RunPod REST + the pod's HTTP server, drives the full CLI flow.

---

## TDD plan — task-by-task

### Task 1: progress_callback hook in download_handler
**Repo:** remote-comfy-gen-handler (`serverless-runtime/`)
- [ ] Write 3 failing tests for `download_handler.handle(job, progress_callback=cb)`:
  1. callback receives `download_start` before aria2c invoked
  2. callback receives `download_done` after sha256 verify with `{cached, sha256}`
  3. callback NOT called when not provided (existing behavior — already covered by 16 existing tests, just re-verify)
- [ ] Run pytest, expect failures
- [ ] Add `progress_callback` parameter, sprinkle callback invocations alongside existing `_send_progress` calls
- [ ] All 19 tests (16 + 3) pass
- [ ] Commit

### Task 2: preset_resolver module
**Repo:** remote-comfy-gen-handler
- [ ] Write failing tests for `resolve_preset` and `preset_to_download_batch`:
  1. happy path: mocked urlopen returns manifest+preset → returns parsed preset
  2. preset_id not in manifest → raises `KeyError`
  3. malformed manifest JSON → raises
  4. preset_to_download_batch translates `{models: [{url, dest, sha256}]}` → handler batch shape
- [ ] Implement, lift the logic out of `install-entrypoint.sh` verbatim
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] **Verify the legacy one-shot entrypoint still works** — bash dry-run with mocked curl (or just trust that the resolver returns the same dict the inline python did)
- [ ] Commit

### Task 3: installer_server
**Repo:** remote-comfy-gen-handler
- [ ] Write failing tests using aiohttp's test client:
  1. `GET /health` returns `{ok: true, version, ready: true}` with no auth
  2. `GET /volume_info` without token → 401
  3. `GET /volume_info` with valid token → delegates to handler
  4. `POST /install/qwen-image-lighting` with token → SSE stream emits preflight_ok → download_start → download_done → install_done
  5. `POST /install/missing-preset` with token → SSE emits `preflight_fail`
  6. `POST /shutdown` with token → returns ok, schedules self-DELETE
  7. Idle watchdog: no request for `INSTALLER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_SEC=1` → server exits
  8. download_handler raises mid-install → SSE emits `install_error` then closes stream
- [ ] Implement the server
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Commit

### Task 4: Dockerfile + entrypoint mode branching
**Repo:** remote-comfy-gen-docker
- [ ] Add `aiohttp` to `pip install` line in Dockerfile.installer
- [ ] Modify `install-entrypoint.sh` to branch on PRESET_ID / INSTALLER_MODE
- [ ] Local bash test: with PRESET_ID set, takes the oneshot path; without, would try to run installer_server (will fail because /runtime not cloned, but the branch decision must be observable from `echo`s)
- [ ] Generate `installer-v5` tag, push, wait for CircleCI green
- [ ] Commit

### Task 5: ComfyGen `install-preset` CLI
**Repo:** ComfyGen (this one)
- [ ] Write failing tests (mocked RunPod REST + mocked pod HTTP server):
  1. happy path: spawn → poll /health (3 retries) → POST /install → SSE stream prints to stdout as JSON lines → /shutdown → exit 0
  2. /health never ready in timeout → exit 1 with clear error
  3. install_error event → CLI exits 1 with the reason
  4. --keep-alive flag → skips final /shutdown call
  5. install-call command (no spawn) → just streams from existing pod
- [ ] Implement
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Update help text per CLAUDE.md "Every CLI command MUST have verbose, detailed --help output"
- [ ] Commit

### Task 6: Live verification
**Steps:**
- [ ] Build `installer-v5` (Dockerfile + entrypoint changes via Task 4 tag push)
- [ ] Spawn a server-mode pod manually via REST, no PRESET_ID env
- [ ] curl `https://<pod>-3000.proxy.runpod.net/health` → 200
- [ ] curl `/volume_info` with `X-Installer-Token` → free bytes
- [ ] curl `-N POST /install/qwen-image-lighting` → stream of SSE events
- [ ] curl `POST /shutdown` → pod 404s within ~5s
- [ ] Run `comfy-gen install-preset --preset-id qwen-image-lighting --volume-id 7etzak7vfp` end-to-end
- [ ] Record numbers: wall time, cold-start window (spawn → /health ready), install time, total cost
- [ ] Close bead with the numbers in notes

---

## Done criteria

- 3 new `download_handler` tests pass + all 16 existing still pass
- 4 `preset_resolver` tests pass
- 8 `installer_server` tests pass
- 5 `install-preset` CLI tests pass
- `installer-v5` image builds clean and routes correctly on PRESET_ID presence
- Live: `comfy-gen install-preset --preset-id qwen-image-lighting --volume-id 7etzak7vfp` runs end-to-end, prints SSE events as JSON lines, exits 0, pod 404s
- Total wall time for cached install < 90s (60s ixd baseline + ~30s server boot)
- Total cost < $0.01

---

## Edge cases (per project TDD doctrine — non-negotiable)

| Edge case | Expected behavior |
|---|---|
| Token missing in request | 401 with `{ok: false, reason: "missing X-Installer-Token"}` |
| Token wrong | 401 same shape |
| Two concurrent `/install` calls on the same pod | Second 409s with `{reason: "install already in progress"}` |
| Preset has no models (workflow-only) | install_done with `files: []`, ok: true |
| Insufficient free space | preflight_fail with `{reason: "need X bytes, have Y"}`; no aria2c invoked |
| aria2c fails mid-download | install_error with stage="download", file_index, partial bytes; pod stays alive so logs are inspectable |
| SHA mismatch | install_error with stage="verify"; corrupt file removed (existing behavior); pod stays alive |
| RunPod proxy returns 502 (cold-boot race) | CLI retries `/health` up to N times before failing |
| Pod self-DELETE fails (RUNPOD_API_KEY missing) | server still exits cleanly; caller responsible for DELETE |
| Idle watchdog fires mid-install | watchdog must NOT fire while a request is in-flight — gate on a "request in progress" flag |
| Client disconnects mid-SSE | server logs disconnect; install continues to completion (don't cancel a multi-GB download because the client tab closed) |
| Same pod, /install called twice serially (multi-op) | both succeed; second one's preflight sees cached files from the first |

---

## BlockFlow integration shape (informational — implementation lives in BlockFlow repo)

Today's preset install row gains:
1. **"Install" button** → BlockFlow backend shells `comfy-gen install-preset --preset-id <id> --volume-id <vid>`, captures stdout (one JSON line per SSE event), pushes events to the browser via existing websocket/SSE channel
2. **Progress bar** — driven by the `download_progress` events (per-file percent + overall progress)
3. **"View live logs ↗" link** — `https://console.runpod.io/pods?id=<pod_id>&inspectorTab=logs` (still useful as escape hatch; the pod_id comes from the spawn response, which CLI can emit as the first stdout line)
4. **On `install_error`**: show error, leave "View logs" link active, pod stays alive (per edge-case table) so the user can debug
5. **On `install_done`**: pod self-terminates ~5s after, UI flips to "Installed"

---

## Cross-references

- **Builds on:** [[remote_comfy_generator-ixd]] (shipped — one-shot pod, the baseline)
- **Related:** sgs-ui-ngz (BlockFlow's UI work — depends on this for the SSE event shape)
- **Won't break:** all existing GPU-worker commands (download/list/hash/delete/health/volume_info/object_info) — server only adds, doesn't replace
notes:
Implementation landed on feature branches across all 3 repos (not yet merged to main):

- ComfyGen: feat/installer-server-remote_comfy_generator-5f2
  https://github.com/Hearmeman24/ComfyGen/pull/new/feat/installer-server-remote_comfy_generator-5f2
- remote-comfy-gen-handler (serverless-runtime): same branch name
- remote-comfy-gen-docker (serverless-docker): same branch name

Tasks 1-5 complete; Task 6 (live verification) DEFERRED — requires manual
RunPod operation. Image tag bump (v5) also DEFERRED until live verification
passes; tagging the docker repo with v5 will trigger CircleCI.

Test counts: 99/99 ComfyGen tests pass (up from 76). Breakdown of new tests:
- download_handler.progress_callback: 3

title: CPU installer pod image (Dockerfile.installer) + download_handler CLI entrypoint
description:
# CPU installer pod image + download_handler CLI entrypoint

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: superpowers:subagent-driven-development. Steps use checkbox syntax. **TDD doctrine in bd memories applies — failing test first, real behavior in tests, edge cases mandatory.**

**Goal:** Eliminate ~$1.50 of GPU billing per wan-animate-sized preset install by routing the download work to a CPU pod instead of the GPU serverless worker. ComfyGen ships a one-shot CPU image; BlockFlow (sgs-ui-ngz) spawns it.

**Architecture:** Tiny `python:3.12-slim` image (~200 MB) with `aria2c + git`. Entrypoint git-clones `serverless-runtime` at boot (same pattern as the GPU image), invokes the existing `download_handler.py` via a new `python -m download_handler` CLI mode. Single source of truth for download logic — same sha256 verify, same aria2c invocation, same dedup as the worker.

---

## Decision tree (locked via session 2026-05-24 grill)

| Q | Decision | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Where install code runs | Reuse `serverless-runtime/download_handler.py` via NEW `if __name__ == "__main__"` CLI | Single source of truth; bug fixes propagate to both worker AND CPU pod |
| Pod lifecycle | One-shot — CMD runs install, exits with handler's status | RunPod stops billing on pod exit; BlockFlow polls exit code for success/fail |
| Trigger | BlockFlow installer spawns per network volume | Multi-DC endpoints need same files on every volume |
| Image home | Second Dockerfile in this repo (`Dockerfile.installer`) | Single repo, single CircleCI project; tag prefix `installer-vN` differentiates from worker `vN` |
| Reporting | RunPod's pod-log API (stdout) | No new infra; BlockFlow polls `/pods/<id>/logs` |

---

## File structure

### NEW: `serverless-docker/Dockerfile.installer`
```dockerfile
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1.7
FROM python:3.12-slim

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/var/cache/apt,sharing=locked \
    --mount=type=cache,target=/var/lib/apt,sharing=locked \
    rm -f /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/docker-clean && \
    apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    aria2 git ca-certificates curl

# No torch, no ComfyUI, no custom nodes. Just python + aria2c + git.
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip,sharing=locked \
    pip install --upgrade pip && \
    pip install runpod  # download_handler imports runpod for progress; install only

COPY install-entrypoint.sh /install-entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /install-entrypoint.sh

CMD ["/install-entrypoint.sh"]
```

### NEW: `serverless-docker/install-entrypoint.sh`
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "[installer] starting"

: "${PRESET_ID:?env var required}"
: "${PRESET_REGISTRY_MANIFEST:=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Hearmeman24/blockflow-presets/main/manifest.json}"
: "${RUNTIME_REPO_URL:=https://github.com/Hearmeman24/remote-comfy-gen-handler.git}"
: "${RUNTIME_REPO_REF:=main}"

# CIVITAI_TOKEN, HF_TOKEN — optional, passed via env
# RUNPOD_API_KEY — not used by installer itself

# Verify volume is mounted + writable
if [ ! -d /runpod-volume ]; then
    echo "[installer] FATAL: /runpod-volume not mounted"
    exit 2
fi
if ! touch /runpod-volume/.installer-write-test 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "[installer] FATAL: /runpod-volume is not writable"
    exit 2
fi
rm -f /runpod-volume/.installer-write-test

# Clone runtime (same pattern as the GPU image's start_script.sh)
git clone --depth 1 --branch "$RUNTIME_REPO_REF" "$RUNTIME_REPO_URL" /runtime
echo "[installer] runtime cloned at $(git -C /runtime rev-parse HEAD)"

# Fetch manifest + preset.json
echo "[installer] fetching preset $PRESET_ID"
PRESET_URL=$(curl -fsSL "$PRESET_REGISTRY_MANIFEST" \
    | python3 -c "import json,sys; m=json.load(sys.stdin); print(next(p['preset_url'] for p in m['presets'] if p['id']==sys.argv[1]))" "$PRESET_ID")
PRESET_JSON=$(curl -fsSL "$PRESET_URL")

# Translate preset.models → download_handler batch
echo "$PRESET_JSON" | python3 -c "
import json,sys
preset = json.load(sys.stdin)
batch = [{'source':'url','url':m['url'],'destination_path':m['dest'],'sha256':m['sha256']} for m in preset.get('models',[])]
print(json.dumps({'input':{'command':'download','downloads':batch}}))
" > /tmp/job.json

# Run download_handler in CLI mode
cd /runtime
exec python -m download_handler --job /tmp/job.json
```

### MODIFY: `serverless-runtime/download_handler.py`
Add at the bottom:
```python
def _cli_main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    """CLI entrypoint for the CPU installer pod.

    Reads a job dict (same shape as the worker dispatch input) from --job FILE
    or stdin, runs handle(), prints the result as JSON, returns 0 on
    {ok: true}, else 1.
    """
    import argparse, sys
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Download handler — CLI mode for CPU installer pod")
    p.add_argument("--job", help="Path to job JSON (omit to read stdin)")
    args = p.parse_args(argv)
    src = open(args.job) if args.job else sys.stdin
    job = json.load(src)
    if args.job:
        src.close()
    result = handle(job)
    print(json.dumps(result))
    return 0 if result.get("ok") else 1


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import sys
    sys.exit(_cli_main())
```

Plus: `_send_progress` currently calls `runpod.serverless.progress_update`. When run via CLI (no runpod harness), that call may raise — current code already has a try/except around it (line ~65), so the CLI path is safe. **Verify the existing try/except actually swallows the relevant exception type** during the CLI test.

### MODIFY: `serverless-docker/.circleci/config.yml` (setup config)
Add a separate filter so tags matching `installer-v.*` route to a different continuation config (or extend `generate_continue.py` to emit a different workflow when CIRCLE_TAG starts with `installer-`).

**Recommended approach:** keep ONE continuation config; branch inside it by tag pattern. The setup job emits BOTH `build_worker` and `build_installer` workflows, each gated on its respective tag prefix.

### MODIFY: `serverless-docker/.circleci/generate_continue.py`
Add a second workflow alongside `build-deploy-smoke`:
```yaml
workflows:
  build-installer:
    jobs:
      - build_and_push_installer:
          context: [docker-hub, flowbot-webhook-token]
          filters:
            tags:
              only: /^installer-v.*$/
            branches:
              ignore: /.*/
```

The `build_and_push_installer` job mirrors `build_and_push` but:
- Uses `-f Dockerfile.installer` in the `docker buildx build` invocation
- Tags as `hearmeman/comfyui-serverless:${CIRCLE_TAG}` (e.g. `installer-v1`)
- Cache target: `hearmeman/comfyui-serverless:installer-buildcache` (separate from worker buildcache)
- No update_endpoint / wait_for_rollout / smoke_test — image is tested by live install runs from BlockFlow

The existing `build-deploy-smoke` workflow keeps its `^v.*$` filter (NOT `^installer-v.*$`) so `installer-v1` doesn't trigger the worker pipeline. **Verify the existing regex `/^v.*$/` doesn't match `installer-v1` — it doesn't, since the regex is anchored at start with `v` not `installer-v`. Confirm in the generated config.**

### MODIFY: `tests/test_download_handler.py`
Add cases for the CLI entrypoint (next section).

---

## TDD plan — task-by-task

### Task 1: download_handler CLI entrypoint
**Files:** modify `serverless-runtime/download_handler.py`, add tests to `tests/test_download_handler.py`

**Steps:**
- [ ] Write failing tests for `_cli_main`:
  1. happy path: job dict from stdin → handle() runs → JSON to stdout → exit 0
  2. job from --job FILE: same, but reads from a file path
  3. handle() returns ok=false → exit 1
  4. malformed JSON in stdin → JSONDecodeError raises (don't catch; let pytest see it)
  5. _send_progress noop when runpod harness absent — runs to completion without error
- [ ] Run pytest, expect failures (function doesn't exist)
- [ ] Implement `_cli_main` + `if __name__ == "__main__"` block
- [ ] Verify _send_progress already has its try/except — read `runpod.serverless.progress_update` call and confirm the exception path is caught even when runpod is uninstalled (it's in a try/except Exception — broad enough)
- [ ] Run pytest, all pass
- [ ] Commit: "Add download_handler CLI entrypoint for CPU installer pod"
- [ ] Push to remote-comfy-gen-handler

### Task 2: install-entrypoint.sh
**Files:** create `serverless-docker/install-entrypoint.sh`

**Steps:**
- [ ] Write the script per the spec above
- [ ] Local smoke (without Docker): set PRESET_ID=qwen-image-lighting + RUNTIME_REPO_URL + use a fake `/runpod-volume` via `mkdir /tmp/fake-volume && PRESET_ID=qwen-image-lighting bash install-entrypoint.sh` (won't download, will fail at git clone or at /runpod-volume not mounted check — which is good; we're testing the script's branching, not the downloads)
- [ ] Test missing PRESET_ID → script exits 1 with "PRESET_ID env var required"
- [ ] Test missing /runpod-volume → script exits 2 with "FATAL: /runpod-volume not mounted"
- [ ] Test PRESET_ID not in manifest → curl/python step fails with StopIteration; script exits via set -e
- [ ] Commit: "Add CPU installer pod entrypoint"

### Task 3: Dockerfile.installer + CI workflow
**Files:** create `serverless-docker/Dockerfile.installer`, modify `serverless-docker/.circleci/generate_continue.py`

**Steps:**
- [ ] Write Dockerfile.installer per spec
- [ ] Modify generate_continue.py to emit BOTH workflows; add tag-pattern branching
- [ ] `circleci config validate` on the generated continuation YAML — must pass
- [ ] Local docker build test (skip if no docker locally): `docker buildx build -f Dockerfile.installer -t test-installer .` — expect ~200 MB image, exits clean
- [ ] Commit + push to serverless-docker
- [ ] Tag `installer-v1` + push tag

### Task 4: Live verification (manual)
**Steps:**
- [ ] Wait for CircleCI `build-installer` workflow to complete → `hearmeman/comfyui-serverless:installer-v1` lives on Docker Hub
- [ ] Manually spawn a CPU pod via RunPod GraphQL (one-shot, with the image + qwen-image-lighting preset). Recipe to record in bead notes:
  ```graphql
  mutation { podFindAndDeployOnDemand(input: {
    cloudType: COMMUNITY, gpuCount: 0, minVcpuCount: 2, minMemoryInGb: 4,
    networkVolumeId: "7etzak7vfp",
    containerDiskInGb: 10,
    imageName: "hearmeman/comfyui-serverless:installer-v1",
    env: [
      {key: "PRESET_ID", value: "qwen-image-lighting"},
      {key: "CIVITAI_TOKEN", value: "..."},
    ]
  }) { id imageName } }
  ```
- [ ] Poll the pod's logs via RunPod /pods/<id>/logs every 10s until pod exits
- [ ] Verify exit code 0 (or read it from pod status)
- [ ] Verify all 4 qwen models are on the volume by submitting a fresh `comfy-gen list diffusion_models --endpoint-id 5fkobbksgrtww6` (or by spawning a brief check pod that ls's `/runpod-volume/ComfyUI/models/`)
- [ ] Record total elapsed time + cost — should be ~5 min and ~$0.05 (the qwen preset is ~52GB cached, but if volume already has them, 0 downloads → ~30s)
- [ ] For a real cost demo: pick a NOT-already-installed preset (or fresh volume), run install, time it
- [ ] Close bead with the numbers recorded in notes

---

## Done criteria (verification target)

- 5 unit tests for `_cli_main` pass (stdin path, file path, exit-1 path, edge cases)
- Dockerfile.installer builds cleanly, image is < 300 MB
- CircleCI `build-installer` workflow runs green on `installer-v1` tag
- Live: spawning the pod with PRESET_ID=qwen-image-lighting downloads all 4 models to /runpod-volume, pod exits 0, total wall time + cost recorded in bead notes
- A second tag (`installer-v2`) builds in < 1 min via cache (proves cache works)

---

## Edge cases to handle (per project TDD doctrine — non-negotiable)

| Edge case | Expected behavior |
|---|---|
| PRESET_ID not in manifest | entrypoint exits non-zero with clear error |
| /runpod-volume not mounted | exits 2 with "volume not mounted" |
| /runpod-volume not writable | exits 2 with "volume not writable" |
| CIVITAI_TOKEN required by a preset model but absent | handler raises clean error; pod exits non-zero |
| Network failure mid-download | aria2c retries (built-in); on final fail handle() returns ok=false, exits 1 |
| sha256 mismatch on downloaded file | handle() removes the corrupt file, returns ok=false, exits 1 |
| Preset has 0 models (workflow-only) | handle() returns ok=true, exits 0 (no-op) |
| Volume has the model already with matching sha256 | dedup hits; aria2c not invoked; result shows `cached: true` |
| Preset.json malformed | curl succeeds; python translation fails; pod exits non-zero |
| RUNTIME_REPO_REF doesn't exist (typo) | git clone fails; pod exits non-zero |

---

## Cross-references

- **Blocks:** sgs-ui-ngz (BlockFlow installer needs this image to exist on Docker Hub before it can spawn it)
- **Related code:** existing `serverless-docker/start_script.sh` (the GPU image's runtime clone pattern — mirror it in install-entrypoint.sh)
- **Existing tests to NOT break:** all of `tests/test_download_handler.py` (the new __main__ entrypoint must not change worker-dispatch behavior)

title: [smoke gate] Handle multi-workflow presets + smoke_inputs (wan-animate)
description:
Today smoke_preset.py:106 hardcodes preset["workflow"]["url"] (singular). Crashes immediately on wan-animate, which uses preset["workflows"][] (array, 3 entries). Also doesn't pass file_inputs to comfy-gen submit, so workflows that need image/video files would fail at LoadImage/VHS_LoadVideo even if it got past the schema crash.

**Locked design (from session 2026-05-23):**
- Multi-workflow handling: run only the FIRST listed workflow per preset (cheapest; partial coverage). For wan-animate that's 'Keep Background', which matches preset.tested_against.note.
- Input files: preset declares per-workflow smoke_inputs (schema work in sgs-ui-5ir)
- Cost: full matrix every release (wan-animate runs every tag-push)

**Smoke schema handling (in smoke_preset.py):**
1. `workflow_entry = preset.get('workflow') or preset['workflows'][0]` — support both shapes
2. If `workflow_entry.smoke_inputs` present: fetch each fixture (with sha256 verify), build file_inputs dict keyed by node_id, pass via `comfy-gen submit --file-inputs <json>` (CLI may need this flag added)
3. If smoke_inputs absent on a workflow that needs files: smoke should FAIL with a clear error ("workflow has LoadImage/VHS_LoadVideo but no smoke_inputs declared") rather than crash deep in ComfyUI runtime

**Files to touch:**
- automation/smoke_preset.py — schema handling + file_inputs plumbing
- comfy_gen/cli.py / comfy_gen/serverless.py — verify --file-inputs is exposed at CLI level; if not, add it (currently only the Python API takes file_inputs)
- tests/test_smoke_preset.py — new test file for the multi-workflow / smoke_inputs cases (pure unit tests mocking the file fetches)

**Acceptance:**
- wan-animate preset's 'Keep Background' workflow runs cleanly in v23 smoke matrix (with sgs-ui-5ir landed)
- qwen-image-lighting still works (regression: legacy singular schema)
- Unit tests cover: legacy preset.workflow shape, new preset.workflows[0] shape, smoke_inputs fetched + passed to submit, sha256 mismatch errors clearly, missing smoke_inputs on file-requiring workflow surfaces a useful error

**Blocked by:** sgs-ui-5ir (schema field + wan-animate preset update + fixtures committed)

title: [perf] Registry-backed BuildKit cache + cache mounts + layer reorder
description:
Take the highest-impact items from the audit: ~13 min build → ~3 min on no-op, ~6 min on typical 1-node bumps.

**Items in scope (audit's PR1):**
1. Switch `docker build` → `docker buildx build --cache-to=type=registry,ref=docker.io/hearmeman/comfyui-serverless:buildcache,mode=max --cache-from=type=registry,ref=docker.io/hearmeman/comfyui-serverless:buildcache --push` in generate_continue.py
2. Add `# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1.4` to Dockerfile + `RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip,sharing=locked` on pip steps (ComfyUI core requirements, sageattention, runpod/boto3, the 28-node loop)
3. Reorder layers: put least-changing COPY operations first (start_script.sh, log_forwarder.py, extra_model_paths.yaml after the verify import — currently they invalidate verify)
4. Polish: `--depth 1` on ComfyUI core + node clones, `--filter=blob:none` fallback, `--no-cache-dir` on pip calls

**Out of scope (separate beads if needed):**
- Per-node RUN layers (audit item #4 — moderate complexity)
- Multi-stage build to cudnn-runtime base (audit item #5 — careful smoke needed)

**Acceptance:**
- v21 first-build populates the registry cache successfully
- v22 no-op rebuild (same lockfile) finishes <5 min
- Smoke matrix still green
- Dockerfile validated via `docker buildx build --dry-run` locally OR by the actual v21 build

**Reference:** see audit report from session 2026-05-23 — registry cache is the biggest single win; DLC explicitly NOT worth it.
notes:
v21 first-build measurements:
- build_and_push: 9.5 min (down from ~13 min)
- image size: 11.3 GB (down from 16 GB, -29%)
- buildcache tag populated on Docker Hub (11.3 GB)
- smoke_test still green

Expected v22 numbers (next no-op build):
- build_and_push: ~3-4 min (cache hit on every layer)
- typical 1-node SHA bump: ~6 min (apt/comfy/sage/runpod cached, only node-loop re-runs)

Image-size win (-4.7 GB) was an unintended bonus from --filter=blob:none + apt cache mounts. Will speed up every RunPod cold-start image pull by ~30%.

title: [hotpatch] kijai/ComfyUI-WanAnimatePreprocess ONNX dropdown bug at startup
description:
**Scope:** add hotpatch to serverless-runtime/start.sh that runs before ComfyUI launches:

Detects /ComfyUI/custom_nodes/ComfyUI-WanAnimatePreprocess/nodes.py, checks if already patched (grep filename_list_cache), and if not, injects the fix from upstream issue #32:

      paths, exts = folder_paths.folder_names_and_paths['detection']

title: [smoke gate] Detect when workflow class_types aren't installed in the image
description:
# k5u — smoke-gate workflow pre-validation

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: superpowers:subagent-driven-development. Steps use checkbox syntax.

**Goal:** Catch "class_type not installed" / "Required input is missing" / "Value not in list" failures BEFORE the worker tries to execute, by pre-validating each workflow against ComfyUI's /object_info inside the smoke matrix.

**Architecture:** New `object_info` worker command exposes ComfyUI's INPUT_TYPES; a pure validator in automation/ compares a workflow JSON against that spec; smoke_preset.py calls both between download and submit, fails fast with all problems named. Also adds `comfy-gen object-info` CLI for ad-hoc debugging.

**Tech Stack:** stdlib only (urllib + json), pytest, pytest-mock.

---

## File structure

- CREATE `serverless-runtime/object_info_handler.py` — worker handler (uses info_handler._get_object_info)
- MODIFY `serverless-runtime/worker.py` — add object_info to dispatch block (after volume_info entry)
- MODIFY `tests/conftest.py` — add object_info branch to dispatch_command fixture
- CREATE `tests/test_object_info_handler.py` — 4 cases
- CREATE `automation/validate_workflow.py` — pure validate(workflow, object_info) -> list[str]
- CREATE `tests/test_validate_workflow.py` — 6 cases
- MODIFY `.gitignore` — add `!automation/validate_workflow.py` and `!automation/smoke_preset.py` (latter already tracked — double-check)
- CREATE `comfy_gen/object_info.py` — submit/poll shim (mirror of hash_files.py)
- MODIFY `comfy_gen/cli.py` — add cmd_object_info + parser + dispatcher entry
- MODIFY `automation/smoke_preset.py` — call object_info worker + validate() between download and submit

---

## Dependency DAG

```
Task 1 (worker handler) ───┐
                            ├──→ Task 3 (CLI) ──┐
Task 2 (validator) ─────────┴───────────────────┴──→ Task 4 (smoke integration + live verify)
```

Tasks 1 and 2 are independent — parallelizable.

---

### Task 1: Worker `object_info` command

**Files:**
- Create: serverless-runtime/object_info_handler.py
- Modify: serverless-runtime/worker.py (add dispatch entry alongside the existing hash entry)
- Modify: tests/conftest.py (add object_info branch to dispatch_command fixture)
- Create: tests/test_object_info_handler.py

**Depends on:** none

**Steps:**
- [ ] Write failing handler tests covering:
  1. no filter → returns all classes
  2. filter=["KSampler"] → returns ONLY KSampler (and ok=true)
  3. filter=["NonexistentClass"] → returns {} for classes (ok=true, batch ran fine)
  4. upstream ComfyUI HTTP error → returns {ok: false, error: <msg>}
  Mock urllib.request.urlopen for /object_info; assert handler shape.
- [ ] Run pytest tests/test_object_info_handler.py — expect ImportError or ValueError (handler not wired)
- [ ] Implement object_info_handler.py: handle(job) reads job_input.get("class_types"), calls info_handler._get_object_info() (reuse), filters dict, returns {"ok": true, "classes": ...} or {"ok": false, "error"} on URLError. Wire into worker.py + conftest fixture.
- [ ] Run pytest — expect all 4 pass
- [ ] Commit (runtime repo): "Add object_info worker command for workflow pre-flight"

### Task 2: Pure validator `automation/validate_workflow.py`

**Files:**
- Create: automation/validate_workflow.py
- Create: tests/test_validate_workflow.py
- Modify: .gitignore (un-ignore validate_workflow.py)

**Depends on:** none

design:
Workflow pre-flight validation against ComfyUI's /object_info. Three components:

## Decision tree (locked via grill 2026-05-23)

| Q | Decision | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Worker command shape | NEW `object_info` (not extend query_info) | query_info has different scope (samplers/schedulers/loras); conflating bloats payload + entangles concerns |
| Validation depth | Minimal: class exists + required inputs present + enum values valid | Catches the 3 failure modes actually hit in v18/v19/v20. Type/range checking has false-positive risk (e.g., runtime-resolved values from upstream connections) |
| Wiring scope | smoke_preset.py only (NOT comfy-gen submit) | Matches bead scope; everyday submit stays fast. Submit-side gate can be a follow-up bead if desired later |
| CLI surface | Add `comfy-gen object-info <Class1> <Class2>` | Small (~30 lines, mirrors `comfy-gen hash`), useful for ad-hoc diagnosis of future "Value not in list" issues |

## Architecture

**1. Worker command (serverless-runtime/object_info_handler.py)**

Accepts: `{command: "object_info", class_types?: ["Class1", "Class2", ...]}` (filter optional, omit = all)
Returns: `{ok: true, classes: {<ClassName>: {input: {required, optional}, output, output_name, ...}}}`

Reuses `info_handler._get_object_info()` to fetch /object_info; filters the dict if `class_types` provided. On upstream HTTP error returns `{ok: false, error: <msg>}`.

Wired into:
- `serverless-runtime/worker.py` dispatch block
- `tests/conftest.py` dispatch_command fixture

**2. Pure validator (automation/validate_workflow.py)**

```python
def validate(workflow: dict, object_info: dict) -> list[str]:
    """Return list of human-readable failure strings (empty = workflow is valid)."""
```

Per workflow node:
- If class_type missing from object_info → "Node <id> (<ct>): class not installed"
- For each literal input value (skip lists shaped [node_id, output_idx] — those are connections):
  - If field's spec[0] is a list-of-strings (enum) and value not in it → "Node <id> (<ct>): '<value>' not in <enum>"
- For each declared REQUIRED input in object_info that workflow doesn't provide → "Node <id> (<ct>): missing required input '<name>'"

Pure function, no I/O. Trivially unit-testable.

**3. smoke_preset.py integration**

Between `comfy-gen download` and `comfy-gen submit`:
1. Extract distinct class_types from workflow JSON
2. Call `comfy-gen object-info --class-types <ct1>,<ct2>,...` (or call worker directly)
3. Run `validate(workflow, object_info)`; if failures → raise with the full list
4. Proceed to submit

Smoke job fails fast with all problems named in one error, instead of waiting ~25 min for the worker to hit the same validation and return a cryptic 1-of-N message.

**4. CLI (comfy_gen/cli.py + comfy_gen/object_info.py)**

`comfy-gen object-info <Class1> <Class2> [--endpoint-id ID]` — thin wrapper over the worker command, JSON to stdout. Mirrors the `comfy-gen hash` pattern exactly.

## Done criteria (verification target)

- tests/test_object_info_handler.py: 4 cases — empty filter (returns all), specific filter (returns subset), unknown class (returns empty key, ok=true), upstream ComfyUI 500 (returns {ok: false, error})
- tests/test_validate_workflow.py: 6 cases — clean workflow passes; missing class detected; missing-required-input detected; bad-enum-value detected; connection-input ([node_id, idx]) skipped; multiple failures all reported
- Live: smoke run on qwen-image-lighting stays green (no false positives)
- Live: validator run against user's Wan_Animate.json should report the real bugs (OpenRouterNode.image_resolution and OnnxDetectionModelLoader.yolo_model)

title: CircleCI post-build: rolling release + preset smoke gate
description:
Extend serverless-docker CircleCI workflow with three new jobs after build_and_push:

1. **update_endpoint** — PATCH RunPod template imageName to the new tag (CIRCLE_TAG), triggers rolling release on endpoint 5fkobbksgrtww6
2. **wait_for_rollout** — poll GET /endpoints/{id}?includeWorkers=true until all workers report image ending in :$CIRCLE_TAG and at least workersMin are READY. Timeout ~30 min.
3. **smoke_test** — matrix per preset in Hearmeman24/blockflow-presets manifest. For each preset:
   - Fetch preset.json
   - Reimplement the install flow inline: build downloads.json from preset's models (with sha256), 'comfy-gen download --batch', 'comfy-gen submit' the workflow, GET-Range verify each output URL
   - Pass = all outputs verified within preset.execution_timeout
4. **notify** — FlowBot post (already exists, may need extending to summarize matrix results)

Each job uses 'requires:' so rerun-from-failed re-runs only the failed job — no Docker rebuild on smoke failure (image is already on Docker Hub from build_and_push).

**Decisions locked** (from session 2026-05-22):
- Target endpoint: prod (5fkobbksgrtww6), no ephemeral
- Smoke scope: install + workflow-run + output-URL verify, every preset
- On smoke failure: fail loud, no auto-revert (FlowBot ping + CircleCI red)
- Parallelism: CircleCI matrix per preset (CircleCI Dynamic Config needed since preset list is fetched at runtime from manifest.json)

**New project env vars needed in CircleCI:**
- RUNPOD_API_KEY (for /endpoints/, /templates/ calls)
- CIVITAI_TOKEN (if any future preset uses civitai source — current sole preset 'qwen-image-lighting' is HF-only)
- Maybe HF token for gated HF model URLs

**Cost estimate:** at current 1-preset registry, ~25 min wall + ~$1 RunPod per release. Scales linearly with preset count (matrix parallelism keeps wall time = slowest preset).

title: Add comfy-gen delete CLI subcommand (wraps existing delete worker command)
description:
The `delete` worker command (bead f9d) was added in this session but isn't exposed in the CLI yet. Add a thin CLI shim mirroring `comfy-gen hash`:

- `comfy-gen delete <path>...` (positional) and `--batch paths.json` (array of strings)
- POST to the worker as `{command: 'delete', paths: [...]}`, poll, print JSON result to stdout
- Verbose --help per project convention; document the realpath-based security constraint
- Register in the cli dispatcher

No worker work needed — `delete_handler.py` already exists and is tested.

title: Add hash worker command + CLI for sha256 of existing files (enables client-side dedup)
description:
**Context:** Without an on-demand way to hash files already on the network volume, callers can't decide whether to skip a download. The new download_handler dedup (h3o) needs the caller to supply sha256 — so the caller needs a way to ask 'what's the sha256 of this file you already have?'

**What's needed:**

1. **Worker command** `hash` in `serverless-runtime/hash_handler.py` — accepts `{command: "hash", paths: [...]}`, returns `{ok, files: [{path, sha256, bytes, error?}]}`. Security: same as delete_handler — every path realpath-resolved and must land under /runpod-volume.

2. **CLI subcommand** `comfy-gen hash <path>...` (and `--batch <file.json>` like download). JSON output: `{ok, files: [{path, sha256, bytes, error?}]}`.

3. Wire `hash` into `worker.py` dispatch.

4. Mirror dispatch in `tests/conftest.py` `dispatch_command` fixture.

5. Document in CLAUDE.md Worker Job Commands list + README.

**Acceptance:**
- `command: "hash"` accepts list of paths, returns per-path sha256 + bytes
- Paths outside /runpod-volume rejected (path traversal + symlink escape resolved via realpath)
- Missing file → per-path error, not job failure (idempotent batch)
- Directory path → per-path error
- Streamed hashing (large model files don't blow memory)
- CLI emits valid JSON to stdout, human logs to stderr
- Verbose --help per project convention

title: Add volume_info worker command for disk-space queries (BlockFlow OSS push)
description:
**Context:** BlockFlow's preset installer (`sgs-ui-wisp-las.3`) needs to check ComfyGen's network volume free space before starting a preset install. Each preset's manifest declares a `disk_size_estimate_gb`; BlockFlow blocks install if `estimate * 1.2 > available` and tells the user to uninstall presets in Settings > Storage to free space.

**Current state (audit):** No volume info / disk space query exists. `serverless-runtime/storage.py` handles S3 uploads but doesn't touch the local filesystem for disk metrics. No `df` shell call or `os.statvfs()` anywhere in the runtime.

**Without this:** preset installs run to completion before the user discovers they're out of disk. ComfyGen errors with a generic "no space left" mid-download; partial files litter the volume; BlockFlow's resume-on-partial-fail flow has to clean up afterward. Bad UX, wasted bandwidth.

**What's needed:**
- New file `serverless-runtime/volume_info_handler.py` exposing a `command == "volume_info"` handler.
- Returns `{ok: true, path: "/runpod-volume", size_bytes: <int>, used_bytes: <int>, free_bytes: <int>}` via `os.statvfs("/runpod-volume")`.
- Update `worker.py` (~lines 617-626) to dispatch the new command.

**Acceptance:**
- [ ] `command: "volume_info"` job returns total/used/free bytes for `/runpod-volume`
- [ ] Returns fast (just a syscall; no GPU, no model loading)
- [ ] Documented in ComfyGen docs as part of the worker job command set

**Effort estimate (from audit):** ~1 hour, ~30 LOC

**Cross-project reference:** BlockFlow `sgs-ui-wisp-las.3` (Preset installer). Acceptance criterion: "Disk-space pre-check: query ComfyGen volume free space; block install if `disk_size_estimate * 1.2` > available; show clear 'need X GB, have Y GB' with link to Settings > Storage." Will also surface as a "Storage" UI in BlockFlow Settings showing volume usage.

title: Add delete worker command for path-based file removal (BlockFlow OSS push)
description:
**Context:** BlockFlow's preset installer (`sgs-ui-wisp-las.3`) needs to delete model files from ComfyGen's network volume to support preset uninstall. Without this, disk fills with orphaned models from uninstalled presets, defeating the marketplace UX.

**Current state (audit):** No file-deletion command exists. `worker.py` dispatches only `download`, `list_models`, `query_info`. `download_handler.py` adds files; nothing removes them.

**Without this:** BlockFlow's "Uninstall" button in the preset UI is non-functional, or has to delegate to manual user action via the RunPod console. v1 acceptance criteria for `sgs-ui-wisp-las.3` ("Uninstall: confirm → ComfyGen delete-files command for each path → Settings updated → preset hidden from comfy_gen dropdown") cannot be met without this capability.

**What's needed:**
- New file `serverless-runtime/delete_handler.py` exposing a `command == "delete"` handler.
- Accepts payload: `{command: "delete", paths: ["/runpod-volume/ComfyUI/models/loras/old.safetensors", ...]}`.
- Returns per-path result: `[{path, deleted: bool, error?: string}]`.
- **Security: validate all paths are under `/runpod-volume`** to prevent path traversal (no `..`, no symlink escapes). Reject any path that doesn't start with the volume root.
- Update `worker.py` (~lines 617-626) to dispatch the new command.

**Acceptance:**
- [ ] `command: "delete"` job accepts a list of paths and returns per-path delete result
- [ ] Paths outside `/runpod-volume` are rejected with a clear error (never deleted)
- [ ] Missing files return `{deleted: false, error: "not found"}` (idempotent, no exception)
- [ ] Documented in ComfyGen docs with the security constraint called out

**Effort estimate (from audit):** 1-2 hours, ~80 LOC

**Cross-project reference:** BlockFlow `sgs-ui-wisp-las.3` (Preset installer). Hard dependency for the uninstall flow. Also enables BlockFlow's preset update flow (orphaned-file cleanup before downloading new version) and Storage tab in Settings.

title: Add sha256 verification + content-addressable dedup to download handler (BlockFlow OSS push)
description:
**Context:** BlockFlow's preset installer (`sgs-ui-wisp-las.3`) needs to verify downloaded model files match expected SHA256 hashes (from the preset's `models.json` manifest). Without this, corrupted/partial downloads silently produce broken presets. The installer also wants to skip re-downloading identical files to save bandwidth on update / retry / resume flows.

**Current state (audit):** `serverless-runtime/download_handler.py` already accepts a list of download entries and reports per-file progress (good). But:
- **No SHA256 verification** — files are downloaded via `aria2c` (lines 103-190) but never hashed against expected values. The schema accepts no `sha256` field.
- **No dedup** — lines 44-46 do a pre-download "does file exist?" check (binary), but no content-based dedup. Re-downloads happen even when content is identical.
- **Schema mismatch with BlockFlow manifest:** ComfyGen accepts `{source: "civitai"|"url", url, dest, filename, version_id}` per entry. BlockFlow's preset manifest is `{url, sha256, destination_path}`. BlockFlow's installer will translate, but having ComfyGen accept the preset shape directly is cleaner.

**What's needed:**
- Add optional `sha256` field to each download entry. If present, compute hash post-download (line ~180-190 in `download_handler.py` per audit) and fail the job on mismatch.
- Add a content-addressable cache: before re-downloading a URL whose SHA256 is already known + matches a file on disk, skip and return "cached".
- Optionally: accept `destination_path` as a synonym for current `dest + filename` to reduce translation burden on BlockFlow.

**Acceptance:**
- [ ] Download job accepts `sha256` per entry (optional, backwards compatible)
- [ ] On completion, returns per-file `{path, sha256, bytes, cached: bool}` so the caller can verify
- [ ] If `sha256` provided and post-download hash mismatches, job fails with a clear error (does not leave a corrupt file on disk)
- [ ] If `sha256` provided and a file already exists at the destination with the same hash, return `cached: true` without re-downloading
- [ ] Documented in `serverless-runtime/download_handler.py` docstring + ComfyGen docs

**Effort estimate (from audit):** 3-4 hours, ~150 LOC

**Cross-project reference:** BlockFlow `sgs-ui-wisp-las.3` (Preset installer). Acceptance criterion: "Sha256 verification: each downloaded file's hash compared to manifest expected. Mismatch → preset stays not-installed, error surfaced."

title: Add /health worker command returning version (BlockFlow OSS push)
description:
**Context:** BlockFlow's OSS push needs a worker-level `/health` endpoint that returns the ComfyGen version. This gates preset compatibility (each preset in the upcoming `Hearmeman24/blockflow-presets` registry declares a `comfygen_min_version`; BlockFlow refuses install if the worker is older) and powers the "attach existing endpoint" flow in BlockFlow's Settings page (paste endpoint ID → BlockFlow calls /health to verify reachability + record version).

**Current state (audit):** No worker-level health command exists. `serverless-runtime/worker.py` dispatches only `download`, `list_models`, `query_info`. RunPod's built-in `/v2/{id}/health` only reports worker counts, not the worker's own version. ComfyGen version `0.2.0` lives in `pyproject.toml` but is never exposed at runtime.

**What's needed:**
- New file `serverless-runtime/health_handler.py` exposing a handler that returns `{ok: true, version: "<from pyproject.toml or hardcoded constant>"}`.
- Update `worker.py` (~lines 617-626 per the audit) to dispatch `command == "health"`.

**Acceptance:**
- [ ] `command: "health"` job returns `{ok: true, version: "X.Y.Z"}` where version matches the installed ComfyGen package version
- [ ] Returns quickly (no model loading, no GPU work) so the BlockFlow attach-flow validation is snappy
- [ ] Documented in ComfyGen's README/CLAUDE.md as part of the worker job command set

**Effort estimate (from audit):** 1-2 hours, ~50 LOC

**Cross-project reference:** BlockFlow epic `sgs-ui-wisp-las`, specifically beads `.2` (Setup wizard's attach-existing flow), `.3` (Preset installer's version-gating), `.10` (Preset registry's `comfygen_min_version` field).
notes:
BOOTSTRAPS TEST INFRA — this is the first issue implemented in this repo, so it ALSO carries the responsibility of setting up pytest infrastructure:

1. Add pytest + pytest-mock as dev dependencies (uv add --dev or equivalent)
2. Create tests/ directory + conftest.py with fixtures for serverless-runtime handler tests
3. Write the /health handler test FIRST: 'test_health_returns_ok_and_version()' — should fail because no health command exists yet
4. Watch it fail with the expected error (unknown command)
5. Implement minimal /health handler to make it pass
6. Watch it pass
7. Document the test setup in README + add to ComfyGen CLAUDE.md (if absent)

Subsequent ComfyGen beads (-h3o sha256, -ema volume_info, -f9d delete) all follow the same test-first cycle but inherit the bootstrapped infra.
